title: let _ = &query.key is a misleading no-op comment
description:
webhook.rs line 48:
    let _ = &query.key; // used below via webhook_keys

This line does nothing — query.key is already a field on the WebhookQuery struct and is used directly at line 53 (query.key). The comment is inaccurate (query.key IS used directly, not 'via webhook_keys'), and the let _ binding itself is dead. It appears to be a leftover from a refactor. When removed, the code is cleaner and the actual use at line 53 is self-evident.
close_reason: P1-P3 fixes: unwrap replaced, INSERT OR IGNORE dedup, X-Real-IP, zeroizing noted, prune wired, strip_suffix, SSRF guard, shared client, dead code removed, issue refs added
